title: Validation of Circular RNA (hsa_circ_0012107) Expression Profile as a Diagnostic Biomarker for Diabetic Retinopathy
author: dewedar, doaa
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.instruction: In our collaborative research with MTI University, we are investigating the potential role of abnormal circular RNAs (circRNAs) as diagnostic biomarkers for diabetic retinopathy. This comprehensive study employs a multifaceted approach, integrating diverse bioinformatics tools and experimental techniques. Bioinformatics Approach: Cir2disease, Circfun, CircBank Databases : Identifying and validating circRNAs associated with diabetic retinopathy. Exploring the coding potential of these circRNAs. Obtaining sequence information for in-depth analysis. miR Walk, miRBase, Diana Tool : Analyzing the interactions between circRNAs, miRNAs, and mRNA targets to understand the regulatory networks involved. RNAfold, CircInteractome, String Database : Investigating circRNA secondary structures to predict stability and functionality. Exploring protein-protein interactions involving circRNAs. Identifying RNA-binding protein sites that may influence circRNA function. Circular RNA Interactome, Craft Tool, CircView : Designing specific primers for circRNA amplification and detection. Identifying miRNA target sites on circRNAs to elucidate their regulatory roles. Visualizing circRNA interactions within the cellular context to gain insights into their potential diagnostic utility. Experimental Approach: Sample Collection: Collecting PBMCs from individuals with diabetic retinopathy and healthy controls. RNA Extraction and cDNA Synthesis: Using TRIzol for RNA extraction and PrimeScriptTM RT for cDNA synthesis. Quantification and Microarray Analysis: Employing qRT-PCR and microarray analysis to quantify circRNAs and analyze expression levels. Statistical Analysis: SPSS Software: Conducting statistical analysis, including correlation and independent t-tests, with a significance threshold of p < 0.05. By integrating these diverse bioinformatics tools and experimental techniques, our study aims to uncover the intricate roles of circRNAs in diabetic retinopathy. This approach not only enhances our understanding of circRNA-mediated regulatory mechanisms but also paves the way for the development of novel diagnostic biomarkers. The identification and validation of circRNAs as biomarkers hold significant promise for early detection and improved management of diabetic retinopathy, ultimately contributing to better patient outcomes.
